cases.title: Testing-plan close-time absent finalization row: unresolved enrolled students become Absent
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Close-time absent finalization block applies (FR-09, BR-13, AC-12).
Open class session exists with multiple actively enrolled students.
At least one enrolled student has successful Present or Late attendance.
At least one enrolled student has no successful attendance record (Pending).
steps: Record roster beforeState: per-student attendance statuses and pending count.
Execute session close processing via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close service boundary.
Query AttendanceRecord rows for all enrolled students in the session.
Compare unresolved students against those with prior Present or Late success.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan Close-time absent finalization row: enrolled students without successful attendance are assigned Absent per BR-13; students with existing Present or Late retain their status.

cases.title: M03 close event triggers M05 absent finalizer within DB transaction
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for class_sessions and attendance_records tables.
Open session exists with three enrolled students: one Present via QR check-in, one Pending with failed CheckInAttempt rows only, one Pending with no attempts.
M03 Session Lifecycle and M05 Attendance Ledger modules are wired per module breakdown.
steps: Capture beforeState: session Open; two students Pending, one Present in attendance_records.
Invoke M03 closeSession(sessionId, actor) which emits session-close absent-finalization event to M05.
Query attendance_records table for all enrolled students within the same transaction boundary.
Verify session row state Closed with closedAt persisted.
Confirm prior CheckInAttempt rows for Pending students are not deleted.
cases.expected: M03→M05 module boundary commits Closed session and creates Absent AttendanceRecord rows for both Pending students; Present student remains Present; rejected attempt history is preserved per BR-13 outcome.

cases.title: SM-03 AttendanceRecord transitions Pending to Absent when SM-01 session becomes Closed
preconditions: Open class session exists in integration test database.
Enrolled student has AttendanceRecord in Pending state with no successful check-in.
Session SM-01 state is Open.
steps: Capture beforeState: AttendanceRecord status Pending for student-session pair; session state Open.
Execute close transition on SM-01 ClassSession (Open to Closed).
Capture afterState: AttendanceRecord status and session state.
Verify no illegal intermediate states are committed to persistence.
cases.expected: beforeState Pending transitions to afterState Absent when session beforeState Open becomes afterState Closed; transition occurs only for students without successful attendance per BR-13 guard.

cases.title: Idempotent close applies BR-13 absent finalization exactly once per student
preconditions: Open session with enrolled student in Pending state and no success record.
Idempotency-Key header support is enabled for POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Real database with unique constraint on (classSessionId, studentUserId) attendance records.
steps: Issue two parallel or sequential POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close requests with the same Idempotency-Key.
Query AttendanceRecord count and status for the Pending student.
Verify audit and close summary counts are not duplicated.
cases.expected: Student receives exactly one Absent AttendanceRecord; repeated close calls return current Closed state without duplicate absent rows or inflated summary.absent count per INV-06 idempotency.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close returns 200 with absent summary reflecting BR-13 finalization
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with bearer token and Idempotency-Key header.
Open session exists with enrolled students: two checked in (Present/Late), three without success.
Session was opened via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close with Authorization header.
Inspect HTTP status 200 and standard {data, meta, error} envelope with error null.
Assert data.state is Closed, data.closedAt is non-null ISO-8601 UTC, and data.summary.absent equals count of students without prior success.
Assert data.summary also includes present, late, and manualPresent integer counts per API design §4.3.
cases.expected: Close returns HTTP 200 with data.state Closed and data.summary.absent matching BR-13 auto-absent count; envelope meta includes requestId and timestamp.

cases.title: E2E-07 primary workflow session close finalizes unresolved students as Absent
preconditions: Lecturer opens Scheduled session via POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open (AC-01).
Section has five enrolled students; two complete successful QR check-in (Present/Late).
Three students never achieve successful check-in while session is Open.
Lecturer is assigned to the session class section.
steps: Lecturer opens session and confirms Open state.
Two students call POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken and receive Success outcomes.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Lecturer queries GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for full roster.
Attempt POST /v1/check-ins for one unresolved student after close (BR-02 guard).
cases.expected: End-to-end close assigns Absent to three unresolved enrolled students per BR-13; two checked-in students retain Present/Late; post-close check-in is rejected with SessionClosed and no new success row.

cases.title: Alternate branch preserves Excused and Manual Present students on close
preconditions: Open session exists with four enrolled students all without QR success.
Lecturer pre-applies manual corrections within edit window: one student to Excused, one to Manual Present with required reason (AC-13).
Two students remain Pending with no manual resolution.
steps: Verify pre-close roster shows Excused and Manual Present for corrected students.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Query GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for all four students.
Inspect audit records for manual corrections remain intact.
cases.expected: Excused and Manual Present students are not overwritten to Absent per BR-13 exception; two unresolved Pending students become Absent; manual correction audit trail is preserved.

cases.title: Alternate path: policy auto-close triggers BR-13 absent finalization for unresolved students
preconditions: Open session with effective policy auto-close offset configured.
Enrolled student has not checked in before auto-close trigger.
System scheduler or policy evaluation can transition Open to Closed per BR-21.
steps: Allow session to auto-close when current time exceeds scheduled start plus late window end.
Verify session state is Closed and active QR tokens are invalidated.
Query AttendanceRecord for enrolled student without successful check-in.
Confirm lecturer manual correction path remains available within edit window per BR-13 exception.
cases.expected: BR-21 auto-close branch transitions session to Closed and applies BR-13 absent finalization identically to manual close; unresolved enrolled student receives Absent; manual fallback via BR-14/BR-16 remains available.

cases.title: System-assigned Absent on close writes audit entry with system actor
preconditions: Open session with enrolled student in Pending state and no success record.
Audit logging is enabled for attendance mutations per BR-22.
Assigned Lecturer is authorized to close the session.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by targetType AttendanceRecord and target session.
Locate audit entry for system-assigned Absent transition.
Verify actor attribution, old status, new status Absent, and correlation ID.
cases.expected: BR-13 system-assigned Absent on close is logged as system actor per BR-22 exception; audit entry includes previous status (Pending or null), new status Absent, timestamp, and correlation ID; 100% mutation audit coverage.

cases.title: Close does not downgrade Present or Late success records to Absent
preconditions: Open session with enrolled students holding Present, Late, and Pending statuses.
Present and Late were assigned via successful POST /v1/check-ins (AC-11).
One student remains Pending with only failed check-in attempts logged.
steps: Record attendance statuses before close for Present, Late, and Pending students.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Query attendance records and verify status immutability for success paths.
Confirm Pending-only student receives Absent and failed attempts are retained.
cases.expected: Present and Late records remain unchanged after close per BR-13 guard; only the Pending student without success is assigned Absent; failed CheckInAttempt history for the absent student is not deleted.

cases.title: BR-07 one-success-per-session: students with prior success are excluded from absent finalization
preconditions: Open session with enrolled student who completed successful QR check-in (Present or Late).
Student has exactly one AttendanceRecord success row per DI-04 unique constraint.
Session is ready to close.
steps: Record beforeState: student AttendanceRecord status Present or Late.
Lecturer calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Query AttendanceRecord for the successful student after close.
Replay POST /v1/check-ins with same Idempotency-Key as original success attempt after close.
cases.expected: BR-13 does not assign Absent to student with existing success record; status remains Present or Late; BR-07 one-success-per-session preserved with post-close replay returning SessionClosed not DuplicateCheckIn.

cases.title: Non-lecturer roles denied session close and cannot trigger BR-13 absent finalization
preconditions: Open session exists in lecturer-assigned section with Pending enrolled students.
Student and SystemAuditor users are authenticated.
Assigned Lecturer bearer token is available as authorized control.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
SystemAuditor calls POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Query session state and attendance records after denied attempts.
Assigned Lecturer calls the same endpoint as control and verifies absent finalization.
cases.expected: Student and SystemAuditor receive 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope; session remains Open and no Absent finalization occurs from denied calls; assigned Lecturer control succeeds and triggers BR-13 absent assignment.

cases.title: Student export denied and cross-scope Lecturer cannot view absent roster after close
preconditions: Closed session exists in section A with BR-13 Absent rows assigned.
Lecturer-B is authenticated but not assigned to section A.
Student account is authenticated.
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A closed session Absent rows.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage.
Lecturer-B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for section A closed session.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden.
Lecturer-A calls the same attendance endpoint as scoped positive control and sees Absent rows.
cases.expected: Student institution-wide export denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer denied roster read per BR-19 without data leakage; assigned Lecturer succeeds and sees Closed session with BR-13 Absent assignments.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: post-close check-in returns SessionClosed before duplicate evaluation
preconditions: Closed session exists with BR-13 Absent rows assigned for unresolved students.
Student with prior successful check-in while session was Open.
Student actor is authenticated.
steps: Student submits POST /v1/check-ins after session is Closed with valid qrToken.
Assert error.code is SessionClosed per BR-02, not DuplicateCheckIn per BR-07.
Submit for student without prior success against Closed session.
Assert error.code is SessionClosed, not ExpiredQr or NotEnrolled.
Verify Absent AttendanceRecord from BR-13 is unchanged by rejected attempts.
cases.expected: Validation order per API design §5.1: session state check (BR-01/BR-02) short-circuits before BR-03 through BR-10 including BR-07; BR-13 Absent assignments remain intact after rejected post-close attempts.

cases.title: Lecturer close session UI updates roster with Absent badges for unresolved students
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated on desktop browser (PG-05 session control page).
Open session displays live roster with mix of Present/Late and Pending students.
ConfirmActionModal copy warns about auto-absent on close per FLOW-07.
steps: Navigate to lecturer session detail page and verify Open status badge and active QR panel.
Tap Close attendance and confirm action in ConfirmActionModal.
Observe session status badge changes to Closed and QR panel is hidden.
Review PG-06 roster: Pending students show Absent danger badge; Present/Late rows unchanged.
Verify summary chips reflect updated present, late, and absent counts matching close API summary.
cases.expected: Browser flow reflects Closed state per UI transition rules; unresolved enrolled students display Absent AttendanceStatusCell with danger variant per BR-13; roster summary updates match close API summary; Excused/Manual Present rows if present are not overwritten.
